rulings.Case Title: KAZUKO K. ARTUS VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2022 line 1. PETITIONER KAZUKO ARTUS' NOTICE OF PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE Hearing Required. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT & Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2022 line 15. PLAINTIFF JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED in part and GRANTED, subject to opposition, in part. Plaintiff seeks "summary judgment on the Complaint in favor or Plaintiff and against Defendant Jamica Jackson and Defendant the Estate of Dorothy Johnson." This request is premature as made as there is no answer on file by the second defendant. CCP 1170.7. Plaintiff further seeks adjudication of affirmative defenses asserted by Jamica Johnson. Summary adjudication is GRANTED, subject to opposition. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHN E WAI VS. SERGEY VOSTRIKOV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Summary Adjudication, And Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2022 line 6. PLAINTIFF JOHN WAI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, AND RECONSIDERATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that any of the Notice upon which the Complaint is based support the removal of the occupants (who allegedly did not have a relationship with the Plaintiff and did not have authorization from the tenant-in-possession to move in (Vostrikov Declaration 1:19-20)) involved in this action other than the only named defendant. Hearing Required to address which operative notice terminated the claimed possessory rights of the claimants =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY ZHONG ET AL VS. KIMBERLIN RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2022 line 14. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to properly fill out all portions of UD-100 and UD-101, to attach complete copies of the Notices served on each defendant. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. SFRO 37.9 (n) is not effective until April 2022. The Notice, read as a whole properly informed the tenants regarding their relocation payment rights, including a complete schedule of fees expressly referenced in the body of the Notice. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to provide a courtesy copy if its opposition. Opposition was considered. Failure to comply with SFLR 2.7B in the future will result in motions being treated as unopposed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
